//: The Play eval fixtures — brief+ICP inputs for 5 BHN-seller accounts.
//STATUS: Synthetic but representative. Replace with live-captured brief data before locking floor/ceiling.

sellerICP.sellerDescription: BHN (Blackhawk Network) is a global branded-payment solutions company offering digital reward cards, prepaid cards, and incentive programs that help enterprises drive loyalty, reward employees, and close sales deals.

productCatalog.name: Tango Card
productCatalog.description: Digital reward and incentive delivery platform — send gift cards, prepaid Visa, and charitable donations at scale.

productCatalog.name: BHN Rewards
productCatalog.description: Enterprise reward management platform for employee recognition, sales incentives, and customer loyalty programs.

productCatalog.name: Prepaid Card Solutions
productCatalog.description: Custom branded prepaid Visa/Mastercard programs for employee benefits, rebates, and promotional payouts.

brief.companySnapshot: Marriott International is the world's largest hotel company with 8,700+ properties across 139 countries. They operate 30 hotel brands including Marriott, Sheraton, Westin, and Ritz-Carlton. Revenue of $23.7B (2023). The company is executing a post-pandemic loyalty expansion strategy centered on the Bonvoy program (210M+ members) and is investing heavily in personalized member experiences and digital engagement.

solutionMapping.painRelieved: Fragmented local reward programs with no global consistency

solutionMapping.jobToBeDone: Digital reward delivery for loyalty tier upgrades and promotions
solutionMapping.painRelieved: Physical gift card logistics and expiration waste
solutionMapping.measurableOutcome: Instant digital delivery vs 5-7 day physical

recentSignals: Marriott Q3 2024 earnings: Bonvoy membership up 14% YoY to 210M members — loyalty expansion is top priority
Announced $1.2B technology investment including digital personalization for loyalty members
Hiring: 3 open roles in Loyalty Technology and Rewards Operations
brief.recentHeadlines: Marriott expands Bonvoy partnerships; Q3 RevPAR up 4.3%
brief.growthSignals: International expansion into Middle East and Asia Pacific; luxury tier (Ritz/Edition) growing fastest

publicSentiment.sentimentSummary: Positive — strong recovery narrative; some employee satisfaction concerns at property level

brief.strategicTheme: Bonvoy loyalty expansion and digital personalization at scale
brief.openingAngle: BHN's digital reward delivery removes the friction between Bonvoy milestones and member delight — no physical cards, no expiry waste, instant global delivery to 210M members
brief.sellerOpportunity: Employee recognition for 377K global staff + Bonvoy member incentive delivery

fitScore.label: Strong Fit
fitScore.reason: Marriott's scale (377K employees, 210M loyalty members) maps directly to BHN's enterprise incentive platform. Existing relationship reduces displacement risk.

brief.companySnapshot: Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform that enables businesses to accept payments, manage revenue, and build financial products. Processes hundreds of billions in payments annually. ~$1T in total payment volume (2023). ~8,000 employees. Series I/pre-IPO. Known for developer-first culture and aggressive hiring.
brief.fundingProfile: VC-backed, Series I — $6.5B raised, valued at $65B (2023 secondary)

keyContacts.name: Jeff Becksted
keyContacts.title: Chief People Officer
keyContacts.angle: Scaling engineering culture; global hiring push

solutionMapping.jobToBeDone: Differentiate Stripe's compensation with instant digital perks for 8,000+ engineers
solutionMapping.painRelieved: Generic benefit packages that don't feel like Stripe's culture of craft
solutionMapping.measurableOutcome: Programmable, branded reward delivery aligned with Stripe's API-first ethos

solutionMapping.jobToBeDone: Instant SPIFFs and deal-close rewards for 1,000+ sales team
solutionMapping.painRelieved: Manual Venmo/check processes for ad-hoc incentives
solutionMapping.measurableOutcome: Same-day payout vs 2-3 week check cycle

recentSignals: Stripe laid off 14% of workforce (2023), now rebuilding selectively — People team under pressure to show culture ROI
Announced global hiring push for 2024 across Dublin, Singapore, and NYC engineering hubs
Stripe launched Stripe Issuing for corporate cards — adjacent to BHN's prepaid programs
brief.recentHeadlines: Stripe processing volume hits $1T; IPO speculation continues for 2025
brief.growthSignals: International expansion; enterprise segment (large merchants) growing 40% YoY

publicSentiment.sentimentSummary: Mixed — developer community very positive; layoffs dampened internal morale; rebuilding employer brand

brief.strategicTheme: Rebuilding culture ROI post-layoffs while scaling engineering globally
brief.openingAngle: After the 2023 reduction, Stripe's People team needs to demonstrate retention value fast — BHN's instant digital reward delivery gives engineers the same frictionless experience Stripe gives merchants
brief.sellerOpportunity: Employee retention rewards + sales incentive automation for the global rebuild

fitScore.label: Potential Fit
fitScore.reason: Engineering culture fit is strong but Stripe's VC-backed status and post-layoff budget scrutiny create risk. No confirmed existing BHN relationship.

brief.companySnapshot: Boeing is the world's largest aerospace and defense manufacturer, producing commercial jetliners, military aircraft, and space systems. Revenue of $77.8B (2023). ~170,000 employees. The company is in crisis mode following the 737 MAX door-plug blowout incident (January 2024) and ongoing production quality issues. New CEO Kelly Ortberg appointed August 2024.
brief.fundingProfile: Public (NYSE: BA) — $45B debt load; credit at risk of junk rating

keyContacts.name: Ziad Ojakli
keyContacts.title: EVP Government Operations & Head of HR
keyContacts.angle: Safety culture rebuild — workforce engagement is priority 1

solutionMapping.jobToBeDone: Recognize and reinforce safety-first behaviors across 170,000 manufacturing workers
solutionMapping.painRelieved: Perception that Boeing rewards production speed over quality/safety
solutionMapping.measurableOutcome: Measurable safety milestone recognition tied to behavior change

recentSignals: DOJ investigation into 737 MAX quality failures — Boeing under consent decree
New CEO Kelly Ortberg's first 90 days focused entirely on 'culture of safety and accountability'
Machinists union strike (Sept 2024) — 33,000 workers walked out over pay and safety concerns
brief.recentHeadlines: Boeing crisis deepens; Ortberg vows culture reset; FAA increases oversight
brief.growthSignals: Defense segment growing; commercial recovery contingent on quality fix

publicSentiment.sentimentSummary: Very negative — safety crisis has damaged Boeing brand significantly; internal morale at historic low

brief.strategicTheme: Safety culture reset and workforce trust rebuild under new CEO
brief.openingAngle: Boeing's new CEO has one mandate: make safety the culture, not a checkbox. BHN's recognition platform can make every safety milestone visible and celebrated — turning the cultural shift into something 170,000 workers feel.
brief.sellerOpportunity: Safety-linked recognition program for manufacturing workforce

fitScore.label: Potential Fit
fitScore.reason: Scale and crisis-driven urgency create a real opening for safety-recognition programs. Budget uncertainty from debt load and strike fallout is a risk.

brief.companySnapshot: Chipotle Mexican Grill is the largest fast-casual restaurant chain in North America, with 3,500+ locations and 120,000+ employees. Revenue of $9.9B (2023). Known for high employee turnover challenge (industry average 130% annually) and aggressive unit-growth strategy (opening 300+ new locations per year). Loyalty program (Chipotle Rewards) has 38M members.

solutionMapping.jobToBeDone: Reduce 130% annual turnover by recognizing tenure milestones and performance at 3,500 locations
solutionMapping.painRelieved: High cost of constantly recruiting and training new crew members

solutionMapping.jobToBeDone: Drive repeat visits with instant digital reward delivery to 38M loyalty members
solutionMapping.measurableOutcome: Instant digital delivery vs paper coupons

recentSignals: Chipotle opened 315 new restaurants in 2023 — accelerating to 285-315 in 2024 with Chipotlane drive-thrus
CEO transition (Brian Niccol → Starbucks, Scott Boatwright interim) creates People team review moment
Chipotle Rewards grew 15% to 38M members — loyalty expansion is a board priority
brief.recentHeadlines: Chipotle unit growth accelerating; CEO departure creates leadership gap
brief.growthSignals: International expansion to Canada, UK, Europe; Chipotlane format doubling new unit economics

publicSentiment.sentimentSummary: Positive brand sentiment; frontline worker advocacy for better pay and recognition is a recurring issue

brief.strategicTheme: Aggressive growth + frontline retention during CEO transition
brief.openingAngle: Every new Chipotle opens with the same retention challenge — BHN solves it before the first day.
brief.sellerOpportunity: Frontline recognition at 3,500 locations + Chipotle Rewards digital incentives

fitScore.label: Potential Fit
fitScore.reason: Scale and turnover urgency are ideal. CEO transition creates a window for new vendor relationships. Strong industry fit — restaurants are core BHN vertical.

brief.companySnapshot: Verizon Communications is the largest U.S. wireless carrier with 143M+ subscribers and ~117,000 employees. Revenue of $133.9B (2023). The company is navigating a strategic pivot from pure connectivity to value-added services (Verizon+, myPlan, perks ecosystem) while managing significant 5G debt and declining wireline revenue. Existing BHN customer.
brief.fundingProfile: Public (NYSE: VZ) — investment grade; $149B total debt post-5G buildout

keyContacts.name: Christy Pambianchi
keyContacts.title: Chief People Officer
keyContacts.angle: Existing BHN relationship — expansion into myPlan perks

solutionMapping.jobToBeDone: Reward 117,000 employees across consumer and enterprise divisions with consistent digital recognition
solutionMapping.measurableOutcome: Consolidated recognition platform replacing 3 legacy tools

solutionMapping.jobToBeDone: Add instant digital reward redemption as a myPlan differentiator to reduce churn
solutionMapping.painRelieved: Subscriber churn to T-Mobile's aggressive promotional offers

recentSignals: Verizon launched myPlan in 2023 — modular pricing with perks (Netflix, Disney+, Apple One) — actively seeking new perk partners
Q3 2024: postpaid phone net adds 239K — beating estimates on perks-driven retention
Open roles: Director, Loyalty & Rewards (Basking Ridge, NJ) — actively building out perks team
brief.recentHeadlines: Verizon myPlan driving subscriber growth; 5G monetization on track
brief.growthSignals: Verizon Business (enterprise) growing 6% YoY; myPlan perks ecosystem expanding

publicSentiment.sentimentSummary: Neutral-positive — myPlan messaging resonating; 5G debt load is investor concern

brief.strategicTheme: myPlan perks ecosystem expansion + enterprise loyalty
brief.openingAngle: Verizon is already a BHN customer — the expansion play is myPlan perks: Tango Card as a native digital reward option accelerates the exact subscriber retention Verizon is chasing.
brief.sellerOpportunity: myPlan perks integration (Tango Card) + enterprise employee recognition expansion

fitScore.label: Strong Fit
fitScore.reason: Existing BHN customer with clear upsell vector into myPlan perks. Scale (117K employees, 143M subscribers) and active loyalty investment create immediate urgency.

contamination_notes: Marriott and Stripe are maximally distinct: hospitality vs fintech, 377K employees vs 8K, existing loyalty platform vs post-layoff rebuild. Account B (Stripe) play must contain zero references to Marriott, Bonvoy, Ritz-Carlton, or Marriott-specific signals.
